Title: GAP-001: Enable MFA on AWS root account
Description:
**Root Cause**: AWS root account (992382542028) does not have MFA enabled - CRITICAL security risk.

**Impact**:
- RISK-003 (score 20) - Unauthorized root access
- AWS FTR blocker (security requirement)
- ISO 27001 Annex A.5.17 (Authentication information) non-conformity

**Implementation**:
1. Login AWS root account (992382542028)
2. Navigate: IAM → Security Credentials → MFA
3. Register hardware MFA device (Yubikey or AWS Virtual MFA)
4. Test MFA login
5. Document MFA serial number in SGSI-ASSETS-001

**Validation**:
```bash
aws iam get-account-summary | grep AccountMFAEnabled
# Expected: 1
```

**Evidence**: Screenshot of MFA enabled + AWS Config rule compliance

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/1

**Related Docs**:
- docs/06-implementation-guides/gap-001-enable-mfa-root.md
- SGSI-POLICY-002 (Access Control Policy)
- SGSI-CAR-001 (CAR-001)

Title: GAP-002: Rotate IAM access key older than 90 days
Description:
**Root Cause**: IAM user "tmpsaasboost" has access key last rotated >90 days ago (violates AWS FTR requirement).

**Impact**:
- RISK-006 (score 16) - Long-lived credentials exposure
- AWS FTR blocker (CIS AWS 1.4)
- ISO 27001 Annex A.5.18 (Access rights) non-conformity

**Implementation**:
1. Identify key: `aws iam list-access-keys --user-name tmpsaasboost`
2. Create new key: `aws iam create-access-key --user-name tmpsaasboost`
3. Update secrets in Secrets Manager (if automated deployments use this key)
4. Test new key functionality
5. Deactivate old key: `aws iam update-access-key --access-key-id OLD_KEY --status Inactive --user-name tmpsaasboost`
6. Wait 7 days → delete old key permanently

**Validation**:
```bash
aws iam list-access-keys --user-name tmpsaasboost
# Check CreateDate <90 days
```

**Evidence**: AWS IAM credential report showing rotation date

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/2

**Related Docs**:
- docs/06-implementation-guides/gap-002-rotate-iam-key.md
- SGSI-CAR-001 (CAR-002)
- SOP-004 (Secrets Management)

Title: GAP-003: Implement AWS Config + CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark
Description:
**Root Cause**: AWS Config not enabled → no automated compliance monitoring for CIS Benchmarks (AWS FTR blocker).

**Impact**:
- RISK-011 (score 16) - Configuration drift
- AWS FTR blocker (8 CIS controls failing)
- ISO 27001 Annex A.8.9 (Configuration management) non-conformity

**Implementation**:
**Phase 1: Enable AWS Config**
1. AWS Console → Config → Get started
2. Enable all regions
3. S3 bucket: `twyn-aws-config-logs` (encryption: AES-256)
4. SNS topic: `twyn-config-alerts`
5. Record all resources

**Phase 2: Enable CIS Conformance Pack**
```bash
aws configservice put-conformance-pack \
  --conformance-pack-name cis-aws-foundations-benchmark \
  --template-s3-uri s3://aws-config-conformance-packs/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark-v1.4.0.yaml
```

**Phase 3: Remediate 8 failing controls**
- CIS 1.4: Access keys ≤90 days (GAP-002)
- CIS 1.12: Root MFA (GAP-001)
- CIS 2.1.1: S3 block public access (already compliant ✅)
- CIS 3.1: CloudTrail enabled (already compliant ✅)
- Others: See implementation guide

**Cost**: ~€50/month (AWS Config recording + rules)

**Validation**: AWS Config dashboard showing >90% compliance

**Evidence**: Config compliance report screenshot

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/3

**Related Docs**:
- docs/06-implementation-guides/gap-003-aws-config-cis.md
- SGSI-CAR-001 (CAR-003)
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (Module 10: CIS Benchmark)

Title: GAP-004: Test backup restoration (DR testing)
Description:
**Root Cause**: Backups exist (RDS snapshots, S3 versioning) but restoration has NEVER been tested → RTO/RPO unknown.

**Impact**:
- RISK-017 (score 12) - Backup/DR failure
- AWS FTR blocker (RES_004)
- ISO 27001 Annex A.5.29-5.30 (Backup, DR) non-conformity

**Implementation**:
**Test 1: RDS Snapshot Restoration**
1. Identify latest snapshot: `aws rds describe-db-snapshots`
2. Restore to test instance:
```bash
aws rds restore-db-instance-from-db-snapshot \
  --db-instance-identifier twyn-prod-test-restore \
  --db-snapshot-identifier <snapshot-id>
```
3. Measure RTO (time to restore: target <8h)
4. Validate data integrity (sample queries)
5. Delete test instance

**Test 2: S3 Cross-Region Replication**
1. Verify replication: Check us-west-2 bucket
2. Simulate disaster: Delete object in primary region
3. Restore from replica: Copy back to us-east-1
4. Measure RPO (data loss: target <4h)

**Test 3: EKS Cluster State**
1. Restore from Terraform state
2. Measure time to rebuild cluster

**Schedule**: Quarterly (next: Jun 5, Sep 5, Dec 5, Mar 5)

**Evidence**: DR test report with RTO/RPO measurements

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/4

**Related Docs**:
- docs/06-implementation-guides/gap-004-backup-testing.md
- SGSI-POLICY-005 (Backup & Recovery Policy)
- SGSI-CAR-001 (CAR-004)

Title: GAP-005: AWS Support plan decision
Description:
**Root Cause**: Currently on AWS Developer Support (~$29/month) but AWS FTR recommends Business Support for production workloads.

**Impact**:
- AWS FTR blocker (SUP_001)
- Slower incident response (no 24/7 phone support)
- No access to AWS Trusted Advisor full checks

**Decision Required**:
**Option A**: Upgrade to Business Support
- Cost: $100+/month (3% of AWS spend, min $100)
- Benefits:
  - 24/7 phone/chat/email
  - <1h response for production down
  - Full Trusted Advisor checks
  - Architecture guidance
  - Third-party software support

**Option B**: Stay on Developer Support + document justification
- Cost: $29/month
- Justification: "TWYN has 24/7 on-call DevOps rotation, AWS TAM not required for current scale"
- Risk: AWS FTR may still flag as non-compliant

**Recommendation**: Option A (upgrade) - cost is justified for production biometric data platform.

**Implementation**:
1. AWS Console → Support → Change support plan → Business
2. Update budget (add €100/month = €1,200/year)
3. Configure support contacts (DevOps Lead, Gestor SGSI)

**Evidence**: Screenshot of Business Support active

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/5

**Related**: SGSI-OBJ-001 (Objective 2: AWS FTR)

Title: GAP-006: CEO signature on Information Security Policy
Description:
**Root Cause**: Information Security Policy (SGSI-POLICY-001) is complete but NOT signed by CEO - ABSOLUTE BLOCKER for ISO 27001 certification.

**Impact**:
- ISO 27001 Clause 5.2 non-conformity (MAJOR finding)
- Auditor will FAIL Stage 1 audit without this
- Demonstrates lack of top management commitment

**Implementation**:
1. Schedule 30-min meeting with CEO
2. Present:
   - This status report (show progress)
   - Information Security Policy document
   - Importance of CEO signature (auditor requirement)
3. CEO reviews policy (10 pages)
4. CEO signs:
   - Physical: Print, sign, scan to PDF
   - Digital: DocuSign or Adobe Sign (legally binding)
5. Save signed version: `docs/02-policies/information-security-policy-SIGNED.pdf`
6. Update SGSI-POLICY-001 metadata (approval date, signature)

**Timeline**: URGENT - must complete before Stage 1 audit (Jul 31)

**Evidence**: Signed policy document with CEO signature + date

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/6

**Related Docs**:
- SGSI-POLICY-001 (Information Security Policy)
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (Module 15: Management Review prep)

Title: GAP-007: ISO 27001 certification for Gestor SGSI (Ricardo)
Description:
**Root Cause**: Gestor SGSI (Ricardo Esper) needs formal ISO 27001 Lead Implementer certification to manage ISMS effectively and handle surveillance audits (years 2-3).

**Impact**:
- RISK-015 mitigation (SPOF risk)
- Enables internal audit capability (reduce €10k-15k external consultant costs)

**Implementation**:
1. Select training provider:
   - PECB (€2,400) - recommended

2. Register for 5-day course:

3. Study materials provided (ISO 27001:2022 standard, case studies)
4. Pass certification exam (70% pass rate, 3h written exam)
5. Receive certificate (valid 3 years)

**Optional Add-on**: LGPD DPO Training (2 days, €800) - recommended

**Budget**: €3,200 total (Lead Implementer + DPO)

**Evidence**: ISO 27001 Lead Implementer certificate + LGPD DPO certificate

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/7

Title: GAP-008: Hire Junior DevOps / SRE
Description:
**Root Cause**: DevOps Lead is single point of failure (SPOF) - only person who can deploy, respond to incidents, manage AWS.

**Impact**:
- RISK-015 (score 12) - SPOF risk
- Business continuity threat (if DevOps Lead unavailable)
- Incident response capability limited to 1 person

**Implementation**:
**Phase 1: Job Description & Hiring**
1. Write JD: Junior DevOps Engineer / SRE
2. Required skills: AWS, Kubernetes, CI/CD, Linux, Python/Bash
3. Post on: LinkedIn, AngelList, Portugal Startups
4. Interview process: Technical + cultural fit
5. Salary range: €40k-50k/year (Portugal market rate)

**Phase 2: Onboarding (see SOP-001)**
1. Week 1: Access provisioning, training (Track 1 + Track 3)
2. Week 2-4: Shadow DevOps Lead (runbooks, deployments)
3. Week 5-8: Hands-on tasks (monitoring, small deployments)
4. Week 12: Independent on-call rotation capability

**Phase 3: Knowledge Transfer**
1. Document runbooks (deploy, rollback, incident response, DR)
2. Pair programming sessions (infrastructure as code)
3. Cross-train: Junior can execute 80% of DevOps tasks

**Success Criteria**:
- Junior DevOps can execute 3/5 critical operations independently
- On-call rotation distributed (2 people vs 1)

**Budget**: €50k/year (salary + benefits + training)

**Evidence**: Hire confirmation + 90-day competence assessment

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/8

**Related Docs**:
- SOP-001 (Onboarding)
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (Track 3: Operations)
- SGSI-COMP-001 (Competence Records)

Title: SOP-001: Onboarding and Offboarding Procedure
Description:
**Purpose**: Standard Operating Procedure for securely onboarding new employees and offboarding departing employees.

**Scope**: All TWYN employees (full-time, contractors, interns)

**Key Activities**:

**ONBOARDING (Day 1 - Week 4)**
- Day 1:
  - Welcome email (Gestor SGSI)
  - MFA setup (MANDATORY before access)
  - Sign Information Security Policy acceptance
  - Receive laptop (encrypted disk, screen lock <5min)
- Week 1:
  - Complete Universal Training (Track 1: 4h)
  - Access provisioning (AWS IAM, GitHub, Slack, email)
  - Manager assigns buddy (shadow sessions)
- Week 2-4:
  - Role-specific training (Track 2 or 3)
  - Hands-on tasks (supervised)
  - 30-day checklist review (HR + Gestor SGSI)

**OFFBOARDING (Last Day - 30 days)**
- Last day (within 4 hours):
  - Disable all accounts (AWS, GitHub, Slack, email, VPN)
  - Revoke MFA devices
  - Collect laptop + hardware MFA tokens
  - Delete local credentials
- If access to secrets:
  - Rotate affected passwords (AWS RDS, API keys)
  - Document in NCR if rotation delayed
- 7 days:
  - Remove from access control lists
  - Archive user data (Google Drive, Slack DMs) if LGPD-compliant retention
- 30 days:
  - Final access review (confirm no lingering access)
  - Update SGSI-COMP-001 (mark as "departed")

**Evidence**: Onboarding/offboarding checklist + access review logs

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/9

**Related Docs**:
- docs/03-procedures/sop-001-onboarding-offboarding.md
- SGSI-POLICY-002 (Access Control)
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (integrated)

Title: SOP-002: Change Management Procedure
Description:
**Purpose**: Control changes to production systems to prevent unauthorized or untested changes that could cause incidents.

**Scope**: All production infrastructure (AWS), Face ID Platform API code, critical dependencies

**Key Activities**:

**1. Change Request**
- Requester submits: What, why, impact analysis, rollback plan
- Approval required: DevOps Lead (technical) + Gestor SGSI (risk)
- Emergency changes: Post-approval within 24h (incident response)

**2. Change Types**
- **Standard**: Pre-approved (e.g., code deploy via CI/CD, security patches)
- **Normal**: Requires approval (e.g., infrastructure changes, dependency upgrades)
- **Emergency**: Immediate (e.g., security hotfix, P0 incident remediation)

**3. Testing Requirements**
- Dev environment testing (mandatory)
- Staging environment validation (for infrastructure changes)
- Rollback plan documented + tested

**4. Implementation**
- Execute during maintenance window (if downtime expected)
- Monitor: CloudWatch metrics, error rates, GuardDuty alerts
- Validate: Smoke tests, health checks

**5. Post-Implementation Review**
- Success/failure documented
- If failure: RCA (root cause analysis) + CAR
- Update change calendar

**Evidence**: Change log (GitHub commits, Terraform plans, approval emails)

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/10

**Related Docs**:
- docs/03-procedures/sop-002-change-management.md
- SGSI-POLICY-003 (Incident Response)

Title: SOP-003: Remote Work Security Procedure
Description:
**Purpose**: Ensure secure remote work practices to protect TWYN data and systems when employees work from home/coworking spaces.

**Scope**: All employees working remotely (majority of TWYN workforce)

**Key Activities**:

**1. Device Security**
- Disk encryption: MANDATORY (BitLocker Windows, FileVault Mac)
- Screen lock: <5 min inactivity (automatic)
- OS updates: Install within 7 days of release
- Antivirus: Enabled + updated (Windows Defender, macOS XProtect)

**2. Network Security**
- Wi-Fi: Avoid public Wi-Fi without VPN
- VPN: Use when accessing corporate resources on untrusted networks
- Home router: Change default password, enable WPA3 encryption

**3. Physical Security**
- Clear desk policy: Lock laptop when leaving desk
- Clear screen policy: Privacy screen filter (optional but recommended)
- No sensitive documents visible on video calls

**4. Data Handling**
- Backup: Automatic (Time Machine, OneDrive, Google Drive encrypted)
- USB drives: Encrypt if used (VeraCrypt)
- Print: Avoid printing sensitive docs; if necessary, shred after use

**5. Lost/Stolen Device**
- Report IMMEDIATELY to Gestor SGSI (security@twyn.com)
- Remote wipe: Gestor SGSI triggers (Google Admin, Intune, Jamf)
- Police report: File within 24h
- Incident investigation: NCR + CAR if policy violation

**Evidence**: Device inventory (encrypted disk status), VPN connection logs

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/11

**Related Docs**:
- docs/03-procedures/sop-003-remote-work-security.md
- SGSI-POLICY-001 (Section 15: Remote Work)

Title: SOP-004: Secrets Management Procedure
Description:
**Purpose**: Securely store, rotate, and access secrets (passwords, API keys, certificates) to prevent credential leaks.

**Scope**: All secrets used by TWYN systems (AWS, GitHub, third-party APIs)

**Key Activities**:

**1. Storage**
- MANDATORY: AWS Secrets Manager or Parameter Store (never hardcode in code)
- .env files: MUST be gitignored, encrypted at-rest
- Never commit secrets to Git (use pre-commit hooks: gitleaks, detect-secrets)

**2. Access Control**
- IAM policies: Least privilege (e.g., only Lambda can read specific secret)
- Audit: CloudTrail logs all secret access
- Humans: Use IAM temporary credentials (STS AssumeRole), not long-lived access keys

**3. Rotation**
- RDS passwords: Every 90 days (automated via Secrets Manager)
- API keys: Every 90 days (manual or automated)
- IAM access keys: Every 90 days (enforced via AWS Config - CIS 1.4)
- Certificates: 30 days before expiry (ACM auto-renews)

**4. Emergency Rotation**
- If secret leaked (committed to Git, exposed in logs):
  1. Rotate IMMEDIATELY (<1 hour)
  2. Audit logs: Who accessed with leaked secret?
  3. Revoke old secret
  4. NCR + CAR (root cause analysis)

**5. Pre-Commit Hooks**
- Install gitleaks:
```bash
brew install gitleaks  # Mac
pip install detect-secrets  # Python
pre-commit install  # Enable hooks
```
- Hooks scan for: AWS keys, private keys, passwords, tokens

**Evidence**: Secrets Manager inventory, rotation logs, pre-commit hook config

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/12

**Related Docs**:
- docs/03-procedures/sop-004-secrets-management.md
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (Module 8: Secrets Management)

Title: SOP-005: IAM Access Recertification Procedure
Description:
**Purpose**: Quarterly review of IAM access rights to ensure least privilege and remove orphaned accounts.

**Scope**: All AWS IAM users, roles, policies

**Key Activities**:

**Quarterly Review (Q1: Jan, Q2: Apr, Q3: Jul, Q4: Oct)**

**1. Preparation (Day 1)**
- Export IAM credential report: `aws iam generate-credential-report`
- Download: `aws iam get-credential-report > iam-report-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).csv`

**2. Review Process (Days 2-5)**
- For each IAM user:
  - Still employed? (cross-check with HR)
  - MFA enabled? (MANDATORY)
  - Access keys used <90 days? (rotate if older)
  - Permissions still needed? (least privilege)
- For each IAM role:
  - Still in use? (check CloudTrail last-accessed)
  - Permissions appropriate? (no *:* wildcards)
  - Trust policy correct? (who can assume?)

**3. Remediation (Days 6-7)**
- Remove: Orphaned users/roles (departed employees)
- Rotate: Access keys >90 days
- Reduce: Overly permissive policies
- Enforce: MFA on all users

**4. Documentation (Day 7)**
- Recertification report: Who reviewed, what changed, evidence
- Update SGSI-COMP-001: Access rights per person
- NCR if policy violations found

**5. Approval**
- Gestor SGSI signs off: "Access rights recertified as of [date]"
- Evidence for auditor: Quarterly reports

**Evidence**: IAM credential reports, recertification sign-off emails, remediation tickets

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/13

**Related Docs**:
- docs/03-procedures/sop-005-iam-recertification.md
- SGSI-POLICY-002 (Access Control Policy)

Title: CERT-001: Contract ISO 27001 certification auditor
Description:
**Purpose**: Hire accredited external auditor to perform Stage 1 + Stage 2 audits and issue ISO 27001:2022 certificate.

**Timeline**:
- By Jun 30: Auditor contracted
- Jul 31: Stage 1 audit (documentation review)
- Aug 31: Stage 2 audit (on-site assessment)
- Sep 30: Certificate issued (if pass)

**Requirements**:
- Auditor MUST be accredited by:
  - IPAC (Instituto Português de Acreditação) - Portugal
  - Or INMETRO - Brazil
  - Or IAF member (International Accreditation Forum)
- Experience with tech startups / cloud-native companies
- Fluent in Portuguese (team interviews)

**Vendor Options**:
1. **APCER** (Portugal) - €15k-18k
   - Website: https://www.apcer.pt
   - Pros: Local (Lisboa), tech experience
2. **TÜV SÜD** (Global) - €18k-22k
   - Pros: International recognition, rigorous
3. **BSI Group** (UK/Portugal) - €17k-20k
   - Pros: Invented ISO 27001, premium brand
4. **SGS Portugal** - €15k-19k
   - Pros: Competitive pricing, global presence

**Process**:
1. Request quotes (RFQ) from 3 vendors
2. Compare: Price, timeline, auditor experience
3. Select vendor + sign contract
4. Pay deposit (typically 30% upfront)
5. Schedule Stage 1 (Jul 31) + Stage 2 (Aug 31)

**Budget**: €15k-20k (certification year 1), €3k-5k/year (surveillance audits years 2-3)

**Evidence**: Signed contract, auditor accreditation certificate, quote comparison

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/14

**Related Docs**:
- SGSI-OBJ-001 (Objective 1: ISO 27001 Certification)

Title: TRAIN-001: Deploy Security Awareness Training platform
Description:
**Purpose**: Implement comprehensive security awareness training program to train 100% of employees and reduce human-error risks.

**Platform**: KnowBe4 Security Awareness Training (recommended)

**Implementation**:

**Phase 1: Platform Setup (Week 1)**
1. Contract KnowBe4: €20/user/year × 10 users = €200/year
2. Admin setup: Gestor SGSI as admin
3. Enroll users: Import from HR list (email addresses)
4. Configure SSO (optional): Google Workspace or Microsoft 365

**Phase 2: Training Tracks (Week 2-3)**
1. Universal Training (all employees - 4h):
   - ISO 27001 Awareness
   - LGPD Data Protection
   - Password & MFA Security
   - Phishing Awareness
   - Remote Work Security
2. Role-specific training:
   - Developers: OWASP Top 10, API Security, Secrets (7h)
   - DevOps: AWS Security, CIS Benchmark, EKS, Incident Response (12h)
   - Leadership: ISO 27001 Lead Implementer (40h external certification)

**Phase 3: Phishing Simulations (Month 2+)**
1. Configure monthly simulations (automated)
2. Baseline: Measure initial click rate (expect 15-30%)
3. Target: <5% click rate by Q4 2026
4. Remedial training: Auto-assigned to users who fail

**Phase 4: Tracking & Reporting (Ongoing)**
1. Dashboard: Completion rates, phishing results
2. Quarterly report: Management Review input
3. Evidence: Completion certificates for auditor

**Success Metrics**:
- 100% completion rate (Universal Training)
- <5% phishing click rate (Q4 2026)
- Zero security incidents caused by human error

**Budget**:
- Year 1: €3,500 (KnowBe4 €200 + certifications €3,200 + internal time)
- Recurring: €5,133/year

**Evidence**: KnowBe4 dashboard screenshots, completion certificates

**GitHub Issue**: https://github.com/bekaa-trusted-advisors/TWYN-ISO27001/issues/15

**Related Docs**:
- TRAINING-PROGRAM.md (complete 21-page program)
- SGSI-COMP-001 (Competence Records)
- SGSI-OBJ-001 (Objective 4: Security Awareness Training)
